name: Beaver Harris
abstract: William Godvin "Beaver" Harris (April 20, 1936 – December 22, 1991) was an American jazz drummer who worked extensively with Archie Shepp.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Dewey Redman Quartet

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: Then there’s a record for Indian Navigation. A quartet record with you, Chet, Michael Moore, and Beaver Harris.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: he wrote two songs, and asked me to sing them. And I did these two -- 'Can There Be Peace' and 'I Wish I Knew.'

about_quotes.speaker: Doc Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: And, now I know you made a record with Beaver Harris. ... You did an album with Beaver Harris

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: He had Beaver Harris... and he asked me to get a tenor player... So we went to Toronto... Beaver sit down and I take my (inaudible) the bass
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
